briefdescription.para: Do not map CR or LF.
Map LF to CRLF on output.
Flags for setting shell output newline sequence.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* shell_dynamic_get) (size_t idx, struct shell_static_entry *entry)
memberdef.name: shell_dynamic_get

briefdescription.para: Shell dynamic command descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: Function shall fill the received shell_static_entry structure with requested (idx) dynamic subcommand data. If there is more than one dynamic subcommand available, the function shall ensure that the returned commands: entry->syntax are sorted in alphabetical order. If idx exceeds the available dynamic subcommands, the function must write to entry->syntax NULL value. This will indicate to the shell module that there are no more dynamic commands to read.

briefdescription.para: Filter callback type, for use with shell_device_lookup_filter.

detaileddescription.para: This is used as an argument of shell_device_lookup_filter to only return devices that match a specific condition, implemented by the filter.

simplesect.para: bool, true if the filter matches the device type.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* shell_cmd_handler) (const struct shell *sh, size_t argc, char **argv)
memberdef.name: shell_cmd_handler

briefdescription.para: Shell command handler prototype.

parameterdescription.para: Help printed and command not executed.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* shell_dict_cmd_handler) (const struct shell *sh, size_t argc, char **argv, void *data)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct shell *sh, size_t argc, char **argv, void *data)
memberdef.name: shell_dict_cmd_handler

briefdescription.para: Shell dictionary command handler prototype.

parameterdescription.para: Help printed and command not executed.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* shell_transport_handler_t) (enum shell_transport_evt evt, void *context)
memberdef.name: shell_transport_handler_t

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* shell_uninit_cb_t) (const struct shell *sh, int res)
memberdef.name: shell_uninit_cb_t

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* shell_bypass_cb_t) (const struct shell *sh, uint8_t *data, size_t len, void *user_data)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct shell *sh, uint8_t *data, size_t len, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: shell_bypass_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Bypass callback.
Get by index a device that matches .

detaileddescription.para: This can be used, for example, to identify I2C_1 as the second I2C device.
Devices that failed to initialize or do not have a non-empty name are excluded from the candidates for a match.

parameterdescription.para: the device number starting from zero.
optional name prefix used to restrict candidate devices. Indexing is done relative to devices with names that start with this text. Pass null if no prefix match is required.

briefdescription.para: Get by index a device that matches .

detaileddescription.para: This can be used, for example, to identify I2C_1 as the second I2C device.
Devices that failed to initialize - or deferred to be - are included from the candidates for a match, minus the ones who do not have a non-empty name.

parameterdescription.para: the device number starting from zero.
optional name prefix used to restrict candidate devices. Indexing is done relative to devices with names that start with this text. Pass null if no prefix match is required.

briefdescription.para: Get by index a non-initialized device that matches .

detaileddescription.para: This can be used, for example, to identify I2C_1 as the second I2C device.
Devices that initialized successfully or do not have a non-empty name are excluded.

parameterdescription.para: the device number starting from zero.
optional name prefix used to restrict candidate devices. Indexing is done relative to devices with names that start with this text. Pass null if no prefix match is required.

briefdescription.para: Get a device by index and filter.

detaileddescription.para: This can be used to return devices matching a specific type.
Devices that the filter returns false for, failed to initialize or do not have a non-empty name are excluded from the candidates for a match.

parameterdescription.para: the device number starting from zero.
a pointer to a shell_device_filter_t function that returns true if the device matches the filter.

briefdescription.para: Get a device reference from its device::name field or label.

detaileddescription.para: This function iterates through the devices on the system. If a device with the given name field is found, and that device initialized successfully at boot time, this function returns a pointer to the device.
If no device has the given name, this function returns NULL.

This function also returns NULL when a device is found, but it failed to initialize successfully at boot time. (To troubleshoot this case, set a breakpoint on your device driver's initialization function.)

parameterdescription.para: device name to search for. A null pointer, or a pointer to an empty string, will cause NULL to be returned.

simplesect.para: pointer to device structure with the given name; NULL if the device is not found or if the device with that name's initialization function failed.

briefdescription.para: Get a device reference from its device::name field or label.

detaileddescription.para: This function iterates through the devices on the system. If a device with the given name field is found, this function returns a pointer to the device.
If no device has the given name, this function returns NULL.

parameterdescription.para: device name to search for. A null pointer, or a pointer to an empty string, will cause NULL to be returned.

simplesect.para: pointer to device structure with the given name; NULL if the device is not found.

briefdescription.para: Check if help string is structured help.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to help string or structured help.

simplesect.para: true if help is structured, false otherwise.

inbodydescription.para: Check if what help points to starts with the structured help magic word, but without assuming help is 32 bit aligned, or that if it is a string, that it is at least 4 bytes long.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct shell *sh, const void *transport_config, struct shell_backend_config_flags cfg_flags, bool log_backend, uint32_t init_log_level)
memberdef.name: shell_init

briefdescription.para: Function for initializing a transport layer and internal shell state.

parameterdescription.para: Initial backend configuration flags. Shell will copy this data.
If true, the console will be used as logger backend.
Default severity level for the logger.

briefdescription.para: Uninitializes the transport layer and the internal shell state.

parameterdescription.para: Callback called when uninitialization is completed.

briefdescription.para: Function for starting shell processing.
Function for stopping shell processing.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct shell *sh, enum shell_vt100_color color, const char *fmt,...)
memberdef.name: shell_fprintf_impl

briefdescription.para: printf-like function which sends formatted data stream to the shell.

detaileddescription.para: This function can be used from the command handler or from threads, but not from an interrupt context.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct shell *sh, enum shell_vt100_color color, const char *fmt, va_list args)
memberdef.name: shell_vfprintf

briefdescription.para: vprintf-like function which sends formatted data stream to the shell.

detaileddescription.para: This function can be used from the command handler or from threads, but not from an interrupt context. It is similar to shell_fprintf() but takes a va_list instead of variable arguments.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct shell *sh, unsigned int offset, const uint8_t *data, size_t len)
memberdef.name: shell_hexdump_line

briefdescription.para: Print a line of data in hexadecimal format.

detaileddescription.para: Each line shows the offset, bytes and then ASCII representation.
For example:

parameterdescription.para: Offset to show for this line.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct shell *sh, const uint8_t *data, size_t len)
memberdef.name: shell_hexdump

briefdescription.para: Print data in hexadecimal format.
Process function, which should be executed when data is ready in the transport interface.

detaileddescription.para: To be used if shell thread is disabled.

briefdescription.para: Change displayed shell prompt.

simplesect.para: -EINVAL Pointer to new prompt is not correct.

briefdescription.para: Prints the current command help.

detaileddescription.para: Function will print a help string with: the currently entered command and subcommands (if they exist).

briefdescription.para: Execute command.

detaileddescription.para: Pass command line to shell to execute.
Note: This by no means makes any of the commands a stable interface, so this function should only be used for debugging/diagnostic.
This function must not be called from shell command context!

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the shell instance. It can be NULL when the CONFIG_SHELL_BACKEND_DUMMY option is enabled.

briefdescription.para: Set root command for all shell instances.

detaileddescription.para: It allows setting from the code the root command. It is an equivalent of calling select command with one of the root commands as the argument (e.g "select log") except it sets command for all shell instances.

parameterdescription.para: String with one of the root commands or null pointer to reset.
if invalid root command is provided.

briefdescription.para: Set bypass callback.

detaileddescription.para: Bypass callback is called whenever data is received. Shell is bypassed and data is passed directly to the callback. Use null to disable bypass functionality.

parameterdescription.para: Bypass callback or null to disable.

briefdescription.para: Get shell readiness to execute commands.

parameterdescription.para: Shell backend is ready to execute commands.
Shell backend is not initialized or not started.

briefdescription.para: Allow application to control text insert mode.

detaileddescription.para: Value is modified atomically and the previous value is returned.

briefdescription.para: Allow application to control whether terminal output uses colored syntax.

detaileddescription.para: Value is modified atomically and the previous value is returned.

briefdescription.para: Allow application to control whether terminal is using vt100 commands.

detaileddescription.para: Value is modified atomically and the previous value is returned.

briefdescription.para: Allow application to control whether user input is echoed back.

detaileddescription.para: Value is modified atomically and the previous value is returned.

briefdescription.para: Allow application to control whether user input is obscured with asterisks useful for implementing passwords.

detaileddescription.para: Value is modified atomically and the previous value is returned.

briefdescription.para: Allow application to control whether the delete key backspaces or deletes.

detaileddescription.para: Value is modified atomically and the previous value is returned.

briefdescription.para: Retrieve return value of most recently executed shell command.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct shell *sh, uint8_t *buf, size_t len, k_timeout_t timeout)
memberdef.name: shell_readline

briefdescription.para: Read a line of input from the shell.

detaileddescription.para: This function reads from the shell transport until a newline character is received, storing the data in the provided buffer. The newline character is not included in the buffer. The buffer is null-terminated on success.

simplesect.para: This function should be called from the shell thread in a shell command handler and blocks the thread until a result is returned.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer to store the input line.
Maximum time to wait for a complete line.

simplesect.para: Number of bytes read (excluding null terminator) on success.

parameterdescription.para: If timeout occurred before newline was received.
If buf is NULL or input exceeds buffer size.
If not called from an active shell command or bypass callback is set.

briefdescription.para: Helper macro to create structured help inline.

detaileddescription.para: This macro allows you to pass structured help directly to existing shell macros.
Example:

parameterdescription.para: Command description. This can be a multi-line string. First line should be one sentence describing the command. Additional lines might be used to provide additional details.

Command usage string. This can be a multi-line string. First line should always be indicating the command syntax (without the command name). Additional lines may be used to provide additional details, e.g. explain the meaning of each argument, allowed values, etc.

memberdef.name: SHELL_CMD_ARG_REGISTER
memberdef.initializer: static const struct shell_static_entry UTIL_CAT(_shell_, syntax) = \ SHELL_CMD_ARG(syntax, subcmd, help, handler, mandatory, optional); \ static const TYPE_SECTION_ITERABLE(union shell_cmd_entry, \ UTIL_CAT(shell_cmd_, syntax), shell_root_cmds, \ UTIL_CAT(shell_cmd_, syntax) \ ) = { \ .entry = &UTIL_CAT(_shell_, syntax) \ }

briefdescription.para: Macro for defining and adding a root command (level 0) with required number of arguments.

simplesect.para: Each root command shall have unique syntax. If a command will be called with wrong number of arguments shell will print an error message and command handler will not be called.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a command help string (use SHELL_HELP for structured help)
Number of mandatory arguments including command name.

memberdef.name: SHELL_COND_CMD_ARG_REGISTER
memberdef.initializer: COND_CODE_1(\ flag, \ (\ SHELL_CMD_ARG_REGISTER(syntax, subcmd, help, handler, \ mandatory, optional) \ ), \ (\ static shell_cmd_handler dummy_##syntax##_handler __unused = \ handler;\ static const union shell_cmd_entry *dummy_subcmd_##syntax \ __unused = subcmd\ ) \ )

briefdescription.para: Macro for defining and adding a conditional root command (level 0) with required number of arguments.

detaileddescription.para:
SHELL_CMD_ARG_REGISTER for details.
Macro can be used to create a command which can be conditionally present. It is and alternative to #ifdefs around command registration and command handler. If command is disabled handler and subcommands are removed from the application.

parameterdescription.para: Compile time flag. Command is present only if flag exists and equals 1.
Pointer to a command help string (use SHELL_HELP for structured help).
Number of mandatory arguments including command name.

briefdescription.para: Macro for defining and adding a root command (level 0) with arguments.

simplesect.para: All root commands must have different name.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a command help string. Use SHELL_HELP for structured help.

briefdescription.para: Macro for defining and adding a conditional root command (level 0) with arguments.

parameterdescription.para: Compile time flag. Command is present only if flag exists and equals 1.
Pointer to a command help string. Use SHELL_HELP for structured help.

memberdef.name: SHELL_STATIC_SUBCMD_SET_CREATE

briefdescription.para: Macro for creating a subcommand set.

detaileddescription.para: It must be used outside of any function body.
Example usage: SHELL_STATIC_SUBCMD_SET_CREATE( foo, SHELL_CMD(abc,...), SHELL_CMD(def,...), SHELL_SUBCMD_SET_END )

parameterdescription.para: List of commands created with SHELL_CMD_ARG or or SHELL_CMD

memberdef.name: SHELL_SUBCMD_SET_CREATE
memberdef.initializer: static const TYPE_SECTION_ITERABLE(struct shell_static_entry, _name, shell_subcmds, \ Z_SHELL_SUBCMD_SET_SECTION_TAG(_parent))

briefdescription.para: Create set of subcommands.

detaileddescription.para: Commands to this set are added using SHELL_SUBCMD_ADD and SHELL_SUBCMD_COND_ADD. Commands can be added from multiple files.

parameterdescription.para: Name of the set. _name is used to refer the set in the parent command.
Set of comma separated parent commands in parenthesis, e.g. (foo_cmd) if subcommands are for the root command "foo_cmd".

memberdef.name: SHELL_SUBCMD_COND_ADD
memberdef.initializer: COND_CODE_1(_flag, \ (static const TYPE_SECTION_ITERABLE(struct shell_static_entry, \ Z_SHELL_SUBCMD_NAME(__DEBRACKET _parent, _syntax), \ shell_subcmds, \ Z_SHELL_SUBCMD_ADD_SECTION_TAG(_parent, _syntax)) = \ SHELL_EXPR_CMD_ARG(1, _syntax, _subcmd, _help, \ _handler, _mand, _opt)\ ), \ (static shell_cmd_handler dummy_handler_##_syntax __unused = _handler;\ static const union shell_cmd_entry dummy_subcmd_##_syntax __unused = { \ .entry = (const struct shell_static_entry *)_subcmd\ } \ ) \ )

briefdescription.para: Conditionally add command to the set of subcommands.

detaileddescription.para: Add command to the set created with SHELL_SUBCMD_SET_CREATE.

simplesect.para: The name of the section is formed as concatenation of number of parent commands, names of all parent commands and own syntax. Number of parent commands is added to ensure that section prefix is unique. Without it subcommands of (foo) and (foo, cmd1) would mix.

parameterdescription.para: Compile time flag. Command is present only if flag exists and equals 1.
Parent command sequence. Comma separated in parenthesis.
Pointer to a command help string.
Number of mandatory arguments including command name.

briefdescription.para: Add command to the set of subcommands.

detaileddescription.para: Add command to the set created with SHELL_SUBCMD_SET_CREATE.

parameterdescription.para: Parent command sequence. Comma separated in parenthesis.
Pointer to a command help string.
Number of mandatory arguments including command name.

briefdescription.para: Define ending subcommands set.

memberdef.name: SHELL_DYNAMIC_CMD_CREATE
memberdef.initializer: static const TYPE_SECTION_ITERABLE(union shell_cmd_entry, name, \ shell_dynamic_subcmds, name) = \ { \ .dynamic_get = get \ }

briefdescription.para: Macro for creating a dynamic entry.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the function returning dynamic commands array

memberdef.name: SHELL_CMD_ARG
memberdef.initializer: SHELL_EXPR_CMD_ARG(1, syntax, subcmd, help, handler, mand, opt)

briefdescription.para: Initializes a shell command with arguments.

simplesect.para: If a command will be called with wrong number of arguments shell will print an error message and command handler will not be called.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a command help string. Use SHELL_HELP for structured help.
Number of mandatory arguments including command name.

memberdef.name: SHELL_COND_CMD_ARG
memberdef.initializer: SHELL_EXPR_CMD_ARG(IS_ENABLED(flag), syntax, subcmd, help, \ handler, mand, opt)

briefdescription.para: Initializes a conditional shell command with arguments.

simplesect.para: SHELL_CMD_ARG. Based on the flag, creates a valid entry or an empty command which is ignored by the shell. It is an alternative to #ifdefs around command registration and command handler. However, empty structure is present in the flash even if command is disabled (subcommands and handler are removed). Macro internally handles case if flag is not defined so flag must be provided without any wrapper, e.g.: SHELL_COND_CMD_ARG(CONFIG_FOO, ...)

parameterdescription.para: Compile time flag. Command is present only if flag exists and equals 1.
Pointer to a command help string. Use SHELL_HELP for structured help.
Number of mandatory arguments including command name.

memberdef.name: SHELL_EXPR_CMD_ARG
memberdef.initializer: { \ .syntax = (_expr) ? (const char *)STRINGIFY(_syntax) : "", \ .help = (_expr) ? (const char *)_help : NULL, \ .subcmd = (const union shell_cmd_entry *)((_expr) ? \ _subcmd : NULL), \ .handler = (shell_cmd_handler)((_expr) ? _handler : NULL), \ .args = { .mandatory = _mand, .optional = _opt} \ }

briefdescription.para: Initializes a conditional shell command with arguments if expression gives non-zero result at compile time.

simplesect.para: SHELL_CMD_ARG. Based on the expression, creates a valid entry or an empty command which is ignored by the shell. It should be used instead of SHELL_COND_CMD_ARG if condition is not a single configuration flag, e.g.: SHELL_EXPR_CMD_ARG(IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_FOO) && IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_FOO_SETTING_1), ...)

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a command help string.
Number of mandatory arguments including command name.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a shell command.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a command help string.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a conditional shell command.

parameterdescription.para: Compile time flag. Command is present only if flag exists and equals 1.
Pointer to a command help string.

briefdescription.para: Initializes shell command if expression gives non-zero result at compile time.

parameterdescription.para: Compile time expression. Command is present only if expression is non-zero.
Pointer to a command help string.

memberdef.name: SHELL_CMD_DICT_CREATE
memberdef.initializer: SHELL_CMD_ARG(GET_ARG_N(1, __DEBRACKET _data), NULL, GET_ARG_N(3, __DEBRACKET _data), \ UTIL_CAT(UTIL_CAT(cmd_dict_, UTIL_CAT(_handler, _)), \ GET_ARG_N(1, __DEBRACKET _data)), 1, 0)

briefdescription.para: Initializes shell dictionary commands.

detaileddescription.para: This is a special kind of static commands. Dictionary commands can be used every time you want to use a pair: (string <-> corresponding data) in a command handler. The string is usually a verbal description of a given data. The idea is to use the string as a command syntax that can be prompted by the shell and corresponding data can be used to process the command.

memberdef.name: SHELL_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: static uint8_t _name##_out_buffer[CONFIG_SHELL_PRINTF_BUFF_SIZE]; \ Z_SHELL_LOG_BACKEND_DEFINE(_name, _name##_out_buffer, CONFIG_SHELL_PRINTF_BUFF_SIZE, \ _log_queue_size, _log_timeout); \ Z_SHELL_DEFINE(_name, _prompt, _transport_iface, _name##_out_buffer, \ Z_SHELL_LOG_BACKEND_PTR(_name), _shell_flag)

briefdescription.para: Macro for defining a shell instance.

parameterdescription.para: Logger thread timeout in milliseconds on full log queue. If queue is full logger thread is blocked for given amount of time before log message is dropped.

briefdescription.para: Terminal default text color for shell_fprintf function.
Green text color for shell_fprintf function.
Cyan text color for shell_fprintf function.
Yellow text color for shell_fprintf function.
Red text color for shell_fprintf function.
Print info message to the shell.

detaileddescription.para: See shell_fprintf.

briefdescription.para: Print normal message to the shell.

detaileddescription.para: See shell_fprintf.

briefdescription.para: Print warning message to the shell.

detaileddescription.para: See shell_fprintf.

briefdescription.para: Print error message to the shell.

detaileddescription.para: See shell_fprintf.

briefdescription.para: Command's help has been printed.
Shell API.
